summary: Excellent audience recording captures the legendary 30‑minute Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain jam, with crisp, clean sound and a standout second set.
review: The Wise/Miller audience tape is praised for its crystal‑clear mix that lets the crowd ambience enhance rather than mask the music.  The show shines especially in the second set – a soaring Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain, tight Estimated > Eyes, and a thrilling Other One > Stella Blue sequence – while the first set is noted as uneven but still enjoyable.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and highlight superb sound quality plus iconic second‑set jams; only a few note first‑set shakiness, yielding an overall high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean mix with balanced crowd noise; reviewers call it the best sound for this show and note no major drop‑outs or muddiness.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as uneven with some shaky moments, but the second set delivers a classic arc – a marathon Scarlet > Fire followed by tight transitions into Estimated/Eyes and a powerful Other One > Stella Blue.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note his soaring tone and exploratory solos, especially during the Scarlet‑Fire jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for being upfront and pushing Jerry into great explorations; drops the hammer on Fire.
band_member_comments.Bob: His hoarse vocals are mentioned but also his strong contributions on Big River and slide work on See See Rider Blues.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent is highlighted for flavorful keyboard noodling that sets up the second set, with several reviewers calling it a harbinger of things to come.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mixed remarks – “drummer soup” noted in first set, but solid input from Mickey on Fire and standard‑fare post‑jam drumming.
